Triggering the dual circuit breaker of the incubator park physical space and Insane Scope Stuffing. The hypocritical Apple global supply chain OEM template in the original data has been physically eradicated. This enterprise, named 'Anlite Electronic Technology,' has a business license exceeding 700 words, acting as an extremely absurd heavy-industry encyclopedia: from 'civil aircraft parts production' to 'industrial robot manufacturing,' and crossing over to 'photovoltaic equipment manufacturing' and 'metal processing machinery manufacturing.' The commercial system categorized it under 'Electronic Device Manufacturing' based on these exceedingly hardcore terms. However, the forensic logic engine directly intercepted its fatal flaw: the insured headcount is only 1 person! The paid-in capital is a mere 530,000. Even more ironic are its spatial coordinates: 'No. C-60, Chuangzhi Industrial Park.' Chuangzhi Industrial Park is a typical asset-light software park or enterprise incubator in Nanjing; an address like 'No. C-60' with a letter and two digits usually represents a micro-desk or small cubicle inside an incubator building. The collision of physics and logic completely collapses at this moment: in an incubator desk within an office building, 1 employee holding 500,000 claims to simultaneously establish an aerospace parts production line, an industrial robot workshop, a photovoltaic power generation equipment plant, and a metal machine tool processing center? This is simply a sci-fi spectacle in the history of commercial registration. Stripping away these 700 words of insane padding, the probe locked onto its only possible monetization logic: 'hardware products retail, auto parts retail, software development.' The truth is extremely bare-bones: this is absolutely not a super factory that can shake the global industrial landscape, but a 1-Person Incubator Shell & General Broker. The boss likely just rented a mailbox or desk in the incubator to affiliate the license, usually taking on freelance software jobs online, or acting as a middleman flipping everything from hardware tools to auto parts. The 'Manufacturing Entity' tag bestowed by officials has been subjected to the deepest targeted demolition by the system.
